markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Compile a navigable project wiki from already-built authority: the code index (knowledge_code_nav symbols/references/file-lens), knowledge entities/edges, and rich documents, into wiki projections - the Karpathy LLM-wiki 'knowledge as a compile target' pattern (research basis: .GOV/reference/wp009_karpathy_wiki_research.json; operator decision DEC-004). Raw authority stays in PostgreSQL/EventLedger (the immutable 'raw' layer); the compiled wiki is a regenerable projection (the 'binary'). Bootstrap-scan produces typed pages (module/concept/flow/entity/decision) using token-aware clustering for large repos (FSoft CodeWiki pattern); every page records its citations as precise entity/span/symbol references (richer than file lists); an index/catalog page is generated; EventLedger is the compile log. Pages land in the SAME wiki-projection store as MT-184 (no parallel store) and are never authority.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
The compiled wiki is a projection: deleting every generated page must leave authority byte-identical (negative test required).
Citations must resolve to entity/span/symbol ids + content hashes in authority, not loose file-path strings.
Must reuse the MT-184 wiki-projection store and the existing code-index/knowledge surfaces; no parallel wiki infrastructure.
MUST NOT ship without MT-242 drift/staleness: a compiled wiki without staleness tracking silently lies about a changing codebase (S9.1.1 guard).
scope.expected_behavior: Running the bootstrap compiler over handshake_core itself produces a navigable, cited, typed project wiki (module pages + index) stored as projections, queryable by retrieval for models and renderable by the Notes surface for operators.
acceptance_criteria: Bootstrap over the real handshake_core produces module/concept pages with real citations (entity/span/symbol ids) - no placeholder pages.
Index/catalog page lists all generated pages; EventLedger carries compile receipts.
Deleting all generated pages leaves authority byte-identical (proven negative test).
Real-Postgres integration tests cover compile, citation resolution, idempotent recompile.
verification: cargo test -p handshake_core --features runtime-full,test-utils with real-PG tests for the compiler.
Citation spot-check: a generated module page cites symbols that exist in knowledge_code_nav for that module.
handoff_requirements: List files changed, migrations added, tests run with pass/fail.
Name the exact API/query surface the Notes UI and retrieval consume.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
